summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on Zoning Case ZC 2025-001, a request to rezone parcel 112208B127 from M1 (light manufacturing) to PDM (planned development mixed use) for a 246-unit residential multifamily development with a clubhouse, amenities, and parking. After staff presented a recommendation for denial due to non-compliance with density and setback requirements, and the applicant acknowledged confusion from a prior meeting, the Commission voted unanimously to deny the rezoning request. The Commission also noted the cancellation of a special workshop and scheduled their next meeting for July 1st regarding tax amendments.
decisions: Approved video meeting minutes from June 3, 2025 (Dinwiddie abstained, all others yes)
Denied Zoning Case ZC 2025-001 rezoning from M1 to PDM due to non-compliance with Section 6.02 maximum density of 8 units per acre and Section 6.03 front yard setback requirements (unanimous yes)
Approved motion to adjourn at 7:00 PM (unanimous yes)
action_items: Applicant (PLK Communities / Mick Oaks) to address density and setback compliance issues if they wish to resubmit
Applicant awaiting ODOT traffic report expected second week of July
Director Langenheim to reschedule the canceled special workshop and email commission members availability
Staff to coordinate next meeting on July 1st for tax amendment hearings
key_amounts: 246 apartment/townhome units proposed (later revised to 242 by applicant)
8.55 units per acre proposed vs 8 units per acre maximum allowed
50 foot front yard setback required vs 20 foot setback proposed along Smith Road
Flock safety camera installation cost approximately $1,800
Flock safety camera annual maintenance cost approximately $3,500
notable_topics: Rezoning request from M1 to PDM for multifamily residential development
Density exceeds maximum gross density requirement (8.55 vs 8 units per acre)
Front yard setback non-compliance along State Route 28 corridor
Goshen Police Department request for developer-funded Flock safety camera system
Traffic impact study required by Clermont County Engineer
Loss of M1 industrial-zoned land and impact on township business growth
Confusion between zone change approval and preliminary development plan approval from prior meeting
ODOT traffic report vs full traffic study distinction
Architectural standards compliance and missing elevation drawings
Missing sidewalk connections in townhome areas
notes: There was confusion between the prior meeting's approval scope (zone change only vs. zone change with preliminary plan). The applicant stated the zone change was approved at the June 3rd meeting but was told the preliminary development plan must be approved together with the zone change. The applicant had already reduced units from 274 to 242 in response to earlier feedback. A special workshop scheduled for June 25th was canceled due to Director Langenheim welcoming a new daughter. The next meeting is July 1st for tax amendment discussions. Some informal off-the-record conversation occurred after adjournment.
title: Special ZC 6-24-25
